rows.name: AYCC_Desc
rows.content: Below, and in the following pages, we will collect statistical information about you and your family, like their ages, dates of birth, gender identity, social security numbers, and similar. Each and every piece of information collected is required by the court.

rows.label: What is your gender?
rows.tooltip: This option effects what pronouns we will use for you in your documents. Select 'Male' for He/Him, 'Female' for She/Her, and 'Other' for They/Them. If you use different pronouns, please select 'Other' and contact our office so we can make the necessary changes to the documents.

rows.name: AYP_Desc
rows.content:
The remainder of this questionnaire requests information about your property and your spouse's property. The court requires parties to divorce to make complete disclosure of all of their assets and debts to one another.

As you complete this questionnaire, please respond as to your property. If you know about your spouse's property, please include that information as well. If you do not know about your spouse's property, simply include information about your property.

Please remember, your spouse is required to make full and complete disclosure of all assets, debts, and income. If your spouse is unwilling to disclose such information, and you would like to pursue your right to full and complete disclosure, please contact our office. Our attorneys can help you get the information that you are entitled to. However, this service is not included in our flat-fee uncontested divorce, and it is an indication that your divorce may not be uncontested.

rows.name: AYP_Desc
rows.content:
Marital property is defined in R.C. 3105.171. Generally, marital property is property acquired during the marriage which is owned by either or both spouses and property in which either spouse has an interest.

Separate property is defined in R.C. 3105.171. Generally, separate property is property that was inherited , acquired by one spouse prior to the date of marriage, acquired after a decree of legal separation under R.C. 3107.17, excluded by a valid antenuptial agreement, received as compensation for personal injury, (except for loss of marital earnings and compensation for expenses paid from marital assets), or any gift of property that was given to only one (1) spouse.

rows.name: RE_Heading
rows.content:
Real Estate

Real estate includes, but in not limited to, land, mortgaged properties, building, fixtures, attached to buildings, attached structures (for example, garage, in-ground pool), condominiums, time shares, mobile homes officially converted to real estate, natural condition stakes (for example, gas, oil, mineral rights, existing soil, including trees and landscape), and remainder rights in real estate.

rows.name: RealEstateYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own real estate?

rows.name: REConfirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all real estate property you own. If a real estate is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the real estate. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including real estate properties.

rows.name: NoRealEstateConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any real estate property? This includes any homes, property being rented out, empty land, etc.

rows.name: PropertyInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each piece of real estate owned by you or your spouse:

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.Address
rows.label: What is the address of this property?

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.FMV
rows.label: What is the value of this property?

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.Debt
rows.label: How much is the total debt secured by this property?
rows.tooltip: Please enter zero (0) if there is no debt secured by the property. Total debt includes all loans against the property, including but not limited to, first mortgage, second mortgage, home equity line of credit, etc.

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Owner:
rows.tooltip: If you own a property jointly with a third party (someone other than your spouse), please select your name alone as the owner.

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.SaleKeeper
rows.label: Who will live in this property while the sale is pending?

rows.name: PropertyInfo.{{$index}}.SalePayer
rows.label: Who will pay the expenses related to this property while it is listed for sale?

rows.name: Vehicles_Heading
rows.content:
Titled Vehicles

Title vehicles include, but are not limited to, boats, trailers, automobiles, motorcycles, trucks, mobile homes not officially converted to real estate, golf carts, motor scooters, sports utility vehicles (SUV), recreational vehicles (RV), and all-purpose vehicles (APV).

rows.name: VehiclesOwnedYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own one or more vehicles?
rows.tooltip: "Owned" vehicles includes those owned outright as well as those with debt still owed on them.

rows.name: VehicleInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each vehicle owned by you or your spouse:

rows.tooltip: Vehicle Identification Number/Serial Number

rows.tooltip: To determine the value of your vehicle, please refer to www.nada.com.

addonAfter.name: VehicleInfo.{{$index}}.ValueEstimate
addonAfter.aria-label: Is the value listed for this item an estimate?

rows.name: VehicleInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Owner:
rows.tooltip: If you own a vehicle jointly with a third party (someone other than your spouse), please select your name alone as the owner.

rows.name: HouseholdGoods_Heading
rows.content:
Household Goods and Personal Property

Household goods and personal property include, but are not limited to pets, appliances, electronics, tools, air conditioner window units, doghouses, lawn mowers, above-ground pools, safety deposit boxes furniture, firearms, silverware, collections, china, and books.

rows.name: Business_Heading
rows.content:
Closely Held Stocks & Other Business Interests

If either you or your spouse own a business, or an interest in a business, please list the information requested about the interest below. Any needed equalization of the division of these business interests will be addressed later in this questionnaire.

rows.tooltip: If you do not own 100% of the business, please respond to this question regarding the portion of the business that you or your spouse own only. If the business is partially owned by a third party (someone other than you or your spouse), their interest in the business will not be affected by this divorce. We only need information about your and/or your spouse's interest in the business, whether it be 1% or 100% of the business.

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Value

rows.label: How will this plan be divided?
options: 1/2 of Marital Portion of Account to {{PetFirst}}
rows.tooltip: The marital portion of a retirement account is the amount of contributions to the account during the marriage, plus or minus any market fluctuation on that amount.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.RespDisposition
rows.label: How will this plan be divided?
options: 1/2 of Marital Portion of Account to {{RespFirst}}

rows.name: Maintenance_Heading
rows.content:
Spousal Support

Maintenance is spousal support. Maintenance payments are typically paid monthly by one spouse to their former spouse for a period of months. When there is a large disparity in the parties' income, maintenance may be a consideration for the parties.

rows.name: SpousalSupportYesNo
rows.label: Will you or your spouse be paying spousal support to the other?

rows.name: MaintenancePayer
rows.label: Who will be paying spousal support?

rows.name: MaintenanceType
rows.label: How long will the spousal support be paid?

rows.name: MaintenanceTerm
rows.label: How many months will spousal support be paid?

rows.name: MaintenanceSum
rows.label: What is the MONTHLY amount of spousal support to be paid?

rows.name: MaintenancePaymentMethod
rows.label: How will the spousal support be paid?

options.label: Via Ohio Child Support Payment Central

rows.name: MaintenanceCourtJurisdiction
rows.label: Under all circumstances, the Court shall retain jurisdiction over the issue of spousal support to hear and determine a Motion for Relief from Judgment pursuant to Civ.R. 60(B). On other matters involving spousal support:
options: The Court shall retain jurisdiction to establish or modify the amount and/or duration of spousal support in the event either party files bankruptcy.
The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to establish or modify the amount and/or duration of spousal support in the event either party files bankruptcy.
The Court shall retain jurisdiction to modify the amount of the spousal support order.
The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to modify the amount of the spousal support order.
The Court shall retain jurisdiction to modify the duration of the spousal support order.
The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to modify the duration of the spousal support order.
validators: EXCLUSIVE "The Court shall retain jurisdiction to establish or modify the amount and/or duration of spousal support in the event either party files bankruptcy.;The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to establish or modify the amount and/or duration of spousal support in the event either party files bankruptcy."
EXCLUSIVE "The Court shall retain jurisdiction to modify the amount of the spousal support order.;The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to modify the amount of the spousal support order."
EXCLUSIVE "The Court shall retain jurisdiction to modify the duration of the spousal support order.;The Court shall NOT retain jurisdiction to modify the duration of the spousal support order."

rows.name: MaintenanceArrearage
rows.label: Arrearage or Overpayment
options: Any temporary spousal support arrearage or overpayment shall survive the Judgment Entry
Any temporary spousal support arrearage or overpayment shall not survive the Judgment Entry
